name: Mike Thompson
occupation: Senior Product Manager at Vertex Health (hybrid, 3 days in office)
lifeContext: Mike lives in Frisco, Texas with his wife Sarah (34, freelance UX designer), son Ethan (8), and daughter Lily (3). They moved from Chicago 2 years ago. Mike has high-functioning ADHD; he is highly analytical and prone to intense hyperfocus blocks, often staying up late researching space telemetry, stargazing with his computerized telescope, or coding custom automation scripts. Ethan plays soccer (Frisco FC U-9) coached by Mike, and takes piano. Lily is in daycare. Mike's parents, Tom and Linda, live in Naperville, IL. Sarah's mom Patricia lives in Austin. At work, Mike manages the CareConnect patient portal launch, mentoring junior PM Anika Patel and managing engineering conflicts with VP Greg Holloway. The family is planning a Colorado road trip, adopting a puppy, and refinancing their mortgage. Neighbors Dave and Kim Nguyen are close friends. College buddy Jake Morrison visits yearly. Mike relies heavily on Jarvis (his local OpenClaw AI assistant) to keep track of family routines, manage task lists, and brainstorm technical topics during focus transitions.
companionRelationship: Mike has used Jarvis (local OpenClaw AI) for 4 months as a personal thinking partner. He values Jarvis remembering context across his hyperfocus transitions, helping him manage family calendars, calculate mortgage options, and coordinate coding ideas. Mike starts mornings with a calendar briefing and logs a daily journal entry in the evening.
